title: uxcheckbox
html:
uxcheckbox

Add to My Topics

  • Last UpdatedSept 12, 2025
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The uxcheckbox widget is a Boolean input control. It indicates values of the Boolean field state.

Default Logical Widgets

The default logical widget is ux-Checkbox.

Triggers

(web)trigger onchange: This trigger is fired when the boolean value of the checkbox is changed.

Extends fluent-checkbox Web Component

This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-checkbox. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-checkbox web component.

Keyboard support

When you press the Spacebar key, it toggles/cycles the widget value states.

Layout processing

Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features.

Widget properties

The uxcheckbox widget supports the following properties:
Table 1. Widget Properties
Property Description
html:title Defines a hover over text or tooltip, currently used as helper text.
html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled.
html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only.
html:tabindex Allows or prevents a checkbox from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key.
html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a checkbox.
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class.
label-text Defines a translatable label text.
label-position

Defines the position of the label with respect to its widget. The available options are:

  • before
  • after (default)
Note: If the label-position is not defined for the widget, it is aligned by default after the widget.
tri-state Defines the boolean states for the widget. Assuming the checkbox is initialized with an empty value, the available options are:
  • When the state is true, the widget allows the end user to cycle through the three value states: indeterminate → checked → unchecked → indeterminate → ...
  • When the state is false, the widget allows the end user to cycle through the two value states: indeterminate → checked → unchecked → checked → unchecked → checked → ...
exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid.
Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget.

Helper and validation error visualization

A uxcheckbox deals with a formatting error as well as a validation error at the same moment. In these cases, only one error icon is shown giving the formatting error (tooltip text) message precedence over the validation error.

UI blocking

When the field is in transaction, the uxcheckbox widget blocks user input by becoming read-only.

Widget styling

CSS Selector Selected node(s)
.u-checkbox Widget node
.u-checkbox.checked Widget node when the widget is checked
.u-checkbox.indeterminate Widget node when the widget is in indeterminate state
.u-checkbox .u-error-icon Error icon node
.u-checkbox .u-label-text Label text node
.u-checkbox.disabled or .u-checkbox[disabled] Widget node when the widget is disabled
.u-checkbox.readonly or .u-checkbox[readonly] Widget node when the widget is read-only
.u-checkbox.u-invalid Widget node when the value is invalid
.u-checkbox.u-format-invalid Widget node when there is a format invalid
.u-checkbox::part(control) Control node
.u-checkbox::part(label) Label node
.u-checkbox:active Widget node when the widget is active
.u-checkbox:focus Widget node when the widget has focus
.u-checkbox:focus-visible Widget node when the widget has focus-visible
.u-checkbox:hover Widget node when the widget is hovered

Widget sources

The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/checkbox.

Related Links
  • ux-Checkbox

    This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-Checkbox properties.

  • UX Widgets

    Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder.
